chunk_summary: This section outlines a follow-up reminder system for tele-nutrition, emphasizing closed-loop verification, missed-visit escalation, and pathway-specific follow-up cadences for conditions like malnutrition, anemia, and obesity.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of closed-loop follow-up systems in tele-nutrition workflows.
Learn to design pathway-specific follow-up cadences for various nutritional conditions.
Identify strategies for missed-visit escalation and caregiver confirmation processes.
Apply non-stigmatizing communication in tele-nutrition reminders.
key_facts: Follow-up systems are critical for tele-nutrition as changes occur between calls.
Growth faltering requires a 2-week checkpoint and a 4-week trajectory review.
Acute malnutrition follow-ups depend on risk: weekly or fortnightly weight/MUAC checks.
Anemia follow-ups include early adherence checks and hemoglobin response checks.
Missed follow-ups should trigger reminders within 24–48 hours and phone calls if needed.
Obesity follow-ups include a 4–6 week behavior checkpoint and a 12-week trajectory review.
reference_quote: Follow-up systems are the main safety feature of tele-nutrition because change happens between calls.
document_key_topics: Pathway-specific follow-up cadences for nutritional conditions
